g.text: Algorithm: Pointer Aliasing & SIMD Vectorization Safety

ul.li: Read-After-Write (RAW): Occurs if dst[i] is used as a[i+k] in a subsequent iteration where k < VL.
Vectorization: Compilers like LLVM/GCC insert a Runtime Alias Check if restrict is missing but regions might overlap.
Memory Alignment: Vectorized code often requires 32-byte (YMM) alignment for vmovaps (Aligned) vs vmovups (Unaligned).

ol.li: Range Intersection: Region(dst) ∩ Region(a) = ∅ Region(dst) ∩ Region(b) = ∅

div.p: Lexical Backward Dependency? If overlap exists, is the distance d greater than Vector Length VL? if (dst - a) >= 8 floats: SAFE

g.text: vmovups ymm0, [rsi + rax*4] ; Load 8 floats from 'a'
vaddps ymm0, ymm0, [rdx + rax*4] ; Add 8 floats from 'b'
vmovups [rdi + rax*4], ymm0 ; Store 8 floats to 'dst'
CRITICAL: If (dst < a + n) && (a < dst + n) -> ALIAS DETECTED
